basic_details.job_title: Ordnance Factory Chanda Apprentices Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for 140 Graduate & Technician Apprentice Posts
basic_details.short_information: Ordnance Factory Chanda (OFCH), a unit of Munitions India Limited under the Ministry of Defence, has invited applications for 140 Apprentice posts for one year of practical training under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The vacancies cover 45 Graduate Apprentice (Engineering Streams), 45 Graduate Apprentice (General Streams), and 50 Technician Apprentice (Diploma Holders) positions. Eligible candidates must submit the prescribed application form offline to OFCH, Chandrapur, Maharashtra, on or before 9 July 2026. There is no online application facility for this recruitment.

age_limit.details: Eligibility for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on which of the three apprentice categories a candidate is applying for, since each category requires a different academic background. All categories share common conditions on prior experience and the time gap since qualifying, in addition to a uniform age rule. | Candidates applying under this category must have passed their final year degree in Engineering or Technology in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil discipline from a recognized university. Selection is made purely on the merit of marks scored in the final year degree examination, with no separate written test. | This category is open to candidates holding a Bachelor's degree in Science (B.Sc), Commerce (B.Com), or Computer Application (BCA) from a recognized university. As with the engineering streams, the final selection is decided by the percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying degree examination. | Candidates must hold a Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil Engineering awarded by a recognized State Board of Technical Education. Merit for this category is also determined by the marks secured in the final year diploma examination rather than through any competitive exam. | Across all three categories, candidates must not have had one year or more of job or training experience after completing their qualifying degree or diploma, and they must apply within 5 years of obtaining that degree or diploma. The minimum age limit is 14 years, and the notification specifies no upper age limit for any of the three apprentice categories.

qualification.details: Eligibility for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on which of the three apprentice categories a candidate is applying for, since each category requires a different academic background. All categories share common conditions on prior experience and the time gap since qualifying, in addition to a uniform age rule.

salary_details.details: Apprentices selected under OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 receive a fixed monthly stipend that is shared equally between Ordnance Factory Chanda and the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), Mumbai. The stipend amount depends on whether a candidate is selected as a Graduate Apprentice or a Technician Apprentice. | In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

selection_process: Candidates wondering whether there is a written exam for this recruitment can note that OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 does not involve any written test or interview. Selection is made entirely on the basis of merit of marks obtained in the final year examination of the qualifying Degree or Diploma. After the application window closes
OFCH will scrutinize the submitted forms for completeness and eligibility before preparing a merit list based on academic performance.

Submission of offline application by the candidate within the deadline. Scrutiny of application forms and attached documents by OFCH. Preparation of a merit list based purely on final year Degree/Diploma marks. Verification of original certificates for shortlisted candidates. Joining as an Apprentice for the one-year training period.
how_to_apply: Since OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 accepts only offline applications, candidates must download and fill the prescribed format rather than registering on a web portal. The completed application has to physically reach the OFCH office before the closing date.
Download the prescribed application format from the official website, https://munitionsindia.in.
Fill in personal, educational, and category details in block letters as instructed in the form.
Affix one recent passport-size photograph on the form and keep a second signed photograph loose for submission.
Attach self-attested photocopies of the Date of Birth certificate, Degree/Diploma mark sheets, and category certificate (if applicable).
Sign the declaration section of the application form.

Send the completed application so that it reaches The Chief General Manager, Ordnance Factory Chanda, A Unit of Munitions India Limited, District: Chandrapur, Pin 442501 (Maharashtra), on or before 9 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: Quick Summary: OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates who want the key facts in one place can refer to the summary below before going through the detailed eligibility, stipend, and application sections. This recruitment is for one-year apprenticeship training, not for a permanent job, and selection is based purely on academic merit. The table covers the post category, recruiting organization, total vacancies, last date, and application mode.

rows.details: Ordnance Factory Chanda (OFCH), A Unit of Munitions India Limited, Ministry of Defence
Graduate Apprentice (Engineering Streams), Graduate Apprentice (General Streams), Technician Apprentice (Diploma Holders)

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: The official notification for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 specifies only one critical date for candidates to track: the closing date for submitting the offline application. There is no separate notification date, online registration window, or written exam date mentioned in the source notification, since selection does not involve a written test.

rows.event: Last Date to Submit Offline Application

content_sections.heading: OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details (Post & Trade-wise)
content_sections.text: The 140 total vacancies are spread across three apprentice categories, each further divided by trade or discipline. Graduate Apprentice (Engineering Streams) and Graduate Apprentice (General Streams) each carry 45 posts, while Technician Apprentice (Diploma Holders) carries 50 posts. The trade-wise split below tells candidates exactly how many seats exist for their specific qualification.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit
content_sections.text: Eligibility for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 depends on which of the three apprentice categories a candidate is applying for, since each category requires a different academic background. All categories share common conditions on prior experience and the time gap since qualifying, in addition to a uniform age rule. Candidates applying under this category must have passed their final year degree in Engineering or Technology in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil discipline from a recognized university. Selection is made purely on the merit of marks scored in the final year degree examination, with no separate written test. This category is open to candidates holding a Bachelor's degree in Science (B.Sc), Commerce (B.Com), or Computer Application (BCA) from a recognized university. As with the engineering streams, the final selection is decided by the percentage of marks obtained in the qualifying degree examination. Candidates must hold a Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil Engineering awarded by a recognized State Board of Technical Education. Merit for this category is also determined by the marks secured in the final year diploma examination rather than through any competitive exam. Across all three categories, candidates must not have had one year or more of job or training experience after completing their qualifying degree or diploma, and they must apply within 5 years of obtaining that degree or diploma. The minimum age limit is 14 years , and the notification specifies no upper age limit for any of the three apprentice categories.

content_sections.heading: Category-wise Reservation in OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Reservation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and PwBD (persons with disabilities) candidates is applied separately within each of the three apprentice categories rather than as one combined pool. The table below breaks down how many of the 140 total seats are reserved for each category against the Unreserved (UR) seats.

content_sections.heading: Stipend / Pay Scale for OFCH Apprentices
content_sections.text: Apprentices selected under OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 receive a fixed monthly stipend that is shared equally between Ordnance Factory Chanda and the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), Mumbai. The stipend amount depends on whether a candidate is selected as a Graduate Apprentice or a Technician Apprentice. In-Hand Salary Calculator 2026

content_sections.heading: Selection Process for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering whether there is a written exam for this recruitment can note that OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 does not involve any written test or interview. Selection is made entirely on the basis of merit of marks obtained in the final year examination of the qualifying Degree or Diploma. After the application window closes, OFCH will scrutinize the submitted forms for completeness and eligibility before preparing a merit list based on academic performance. Submission of offline application by the candidate within the deadline. Scrutiny of application forms and attached documents by OFCH. Preparation of a merit list based purely on final year Degree/Diploma marks. Verification of original certificates for shortlisted candidates. Joining as an Apprentice for the one-year training period.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for OFCH Apprentice Application
content_sections.text: Applications submitted without the correct supporting documents are liable to be rejected, so candidates should prepare attested copies in advance. The notification specifies a clear list of certificates and photographs that must accompany the application form. Self-attested copy of Date of Birth proof (SSLC/Matriculation certificate) Self-attested copies of Degree/Diploma mark sheets for all years Self-attested copy of SC/ST/OBC category certificate, if applicable One recent passport-size photograph affixed on the application form One additional loose photograph, signed by the candidate on the front

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Offline for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Since OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 accepts only offline applications, candidates must download and fill the prescribed format rather than registering on a web portal. The completed application has to physically reach the OFCH office before the closing date. Download the prescribed application format from the official website, https://munitionsindia.in. Fill in personal, educational, and category details in block letters as instructed in the form. Affix one recent passport-size photograph on the form and keep a second signed photograph loose for submission. Attach self-attested photocopies of the Date of Birth certificate, Degree/Diploma mark sheets, and category certificate (if applicable). Sign the declaration section of the application form. Send the completed application so that it reaches The Chief General Manager, Ordnance Factory Chanda, A Unit of Munitions India Limited, District: Chandrapur, Pin 442501 (Maharashtra) , on or before 9 July 2026 .

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid While Applying
content_sections.text: Many applications for apprentice recruitments get rejected for avoidable reasons rather than ineligibility. Keeping the following points in mind can help candidates submit a complete and valid application the first time. Assuming an online application option exists — OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 accepts offline applications only. Sending only one photograph and forgetting the additional loose, signed photograph required alongside the form. Submitting photocopies of certificates without getting them self-attested. Applying under a trade or stream that does not match the candidate's actual degree or diploma specialization. Ignoring the experience condition — candidates with one year or more of work experience after qualifying are not eligible. Applying more than 5 years after obtaining the qualifying Degree or Diploma. Posting the application close to the deadline, risking late arrival due to postal delays since the form must physically reach OFCH by 9 July 2026. Claiming SC/ST/OBC reservation benefits without attaching the corresponding category certificate.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the offline application for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 is 9 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 140 total vacancies: 45 Graduate Apprentice (Engineering Streams), 45 Graduate Apprentice (General Streams), and 50 Technician Apprentice (Diploma Holders).

faq.question: Q3. Is the application for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026 online or offline?
faq.answer: The application is offline only. Candidates must send the prescribed form by post or in person to the Chief General Manager, Ordnance Factory Chanda, Chandrapur.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for Graduate Apprentice (Engineering Streams) posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed their final year Engineering/Technology degree in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil discipline from a recognized university.

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility for Technician Apprentice (Diploma Holders) posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must hold a Diploma in Mechanical, Electrical, or Civil Engineering from a recognized State Board of Technical Education.

faq.question: Q6. Is there an age limit for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age limit is 14 years and there is no upper age limit, though candidates must apply within 5 years of obtaining their Degree/Diploma and must not have one year or more of work experience after qualifying.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification does not specify any application fee for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q8. What is the monthly stipend for OFCH apprentices?
faq.answer: Graduate Apprentices (Engineering and General Streams) receive ₹12,300 per month, and Technician Apprentices (Diploma Holders) receive ₹10,900 per month, split equally between OFCH and BOAT Mumbai.

faq.question: Q9. What is the selection process for OFCH Apprentice Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no written exam or interview. Selection is based entirely on the merit of marks obtained in the final year Degree/Diploma examination.

faq.question: Q10. What is the official website for Ordnance Factory Chanda apprentice recruitment?
faq.answer: The official website is https://munitionsindia.in, where the prescribed application format and terms and conditions are available.
